r: dadlānā, dut, dat, dutānā, datnā, ḍāṅṭnā
ety: fr. dut or dat; cf. dutānā; datnā, and ḍāṅṭnā
def: v.t. To rebuke, to chide, to snub.
s.m. A bump or swelling (caused by the bite of an insect, or by bad state of the blood).
s.m. corr. of ḍhanḍhorā, q.v.

ety: dādur, q.v.+ā = Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A kind of song to a quick air (so called from its resemblance to the quick tones of a frog: this class of songs is most common in Agra and Bundelkhand); — the palate, the uvula.

ety: prob. fr. S. दृ, by redupl.
def: s.f. Unripe corn (chiefly barley) which is cut from time to time and brought home to be eaten without being taken to the threshing-floor.
s.f. A cutaneous eruption or rash, herpes, &c. (see dād)
subs: adj. & s.m. Herpetic; — a person having herpes.

def: s.m. Father-in-law of a wife's or husband's father-in-law.
s.m. Name of a famous faqīr
subs: s.m. Name of the sect who are followers of the faqīr Dādū; a member of that sect.
wety: Named after Syed Dad Muhammad Shah Alias Dadu, the founder of the city.

def: v.n. To low, to bellow (as cattle; = ḍakārne, q.v.); to roar; to thunder.
s.m. A frog.

ety: ˚wā = Prk. अओ (with w inserted) = S. क+कः
def: s.m. = dādur, q.v.
s.m. Dowry, dower, portion, nuptial present, marriage-gift ( = dahez).

ety: S. दाम+इका; or fr. dāwan, q.v.
def: s.f. Tying a number of bullocks together for the purpose of treading out grain from the ear; treading out grain by means of a number of bullocks tied together (c.f. dāwan); — untnreshed corn.
s.f. Booming sound (of guns, &c.): — dāʼeṅ-dāʼeṅ s.f. Idem.

ety: i.q. dahine, obl. of dahinā, q.v.
def: adv. To the right, on the right (hand or side)
subs: To dodge right and left and get away
To look right and left, to keep a sharp look out, to be vigilant or careful
v.t. 'To put to the right and left'; to put out of the way, to stow away, secrete, conceal.

def: s.m. = ḍaph, daf, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of دفع 'to push, to repel,' &c.
def: s.m. Pushing, thrusting, beating off; preventing, averting, repelling, repulsion; warding off, preparing
subs: v.t. To push or thrust away or off, to dispel, remove, repel, avert, ward off, parry; to guard (oneself, against, -se)
v.n. To be beaten off, be repelled, &c.; to be turned back; to return.

def: s.m. A kind of bass tambourine (without cymbals); a tambourine.

ety: prob. fr. A. dafʼuṉ, v.n. fr. rt. دفأ
def: s.m. Heat, warmth; ebullition; overflow, excess; disagreeableness, noxious property
subs: (-kā), Disagreeableness (of a thing) to be removed, noxious property to be extracted or destroyed.

ety: for A. دفعة dafʻat, inf. n. of un. fr. دفع 'to push,' &c.
def: s.f. One time, one turn or bout; time; a moment; — a collective body; a class; an item, a section
subs: adv. From time to time, time after time, repeatedly
vulg. dafʻe-dar, s.m. An officer commanding a small body of cavalry or infantry

def: s.m. See s.v. dafʻa.
s.m. pl. (of dafīna), Buried or hidden things, or treasures.
s.m. = dafālī, q.v.
s.m. A man of the class whose business is playing the tambourine; a kind of faqīr who always plays upon the daf or tambourine; a tambourine player (vulg. ḍafālī, ḍapālī).
s.m. = dafālī, q.v.

ety: A. دفر+قلية, inf. n. of un. fr. دفرقليه 'to cook,' &c.
def: s.m. An abominable mess, tasteless victnals, a dish in which much broth, vegetables, &c. are mixed to make it go further, wishwash.
root: د ف ر ق ل ي

ety: A. دفر+قلية, inf. n. of un. fr. دفرقليه 'to cook,' &c.
def: s.m. An abominable mess, tasteless victnals, a dish in which much broth, vegetables, &c. are mixed to make it go further, wishwash.
root: د ف ر ق ل ي

def: s.m. pl. (of dafʻa), Times, &c. (see dafʻa).

ety: for A. دفعةً, acc. of دفعة
def: adv. Time after time, often; — at one time, all at once, suddenly.
s.m. pl. (of daftar), Books; registers; volumes, &c.

ety: act. part. n. of دفع to repel,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Repelling, repellent; — repeller, &c.
subs: s.f. Repelling power; power of expulsion.

ety: for A. دافعة, fem. of dāfěʻ
def: adj. & s.m. Repelling, repellent; — repeller, &c.
subs: s.f. Repelling power; power of expulsion.

ety: for A. دفينة dafīnat, fr. دفين, rt. دفن 'to bury'
def: s.m. A thing buried; buried or hidden treasure; treasure-trove.

ety: formed, after the model of the A., fr. دفع
def: s.m. Repulsion, averting, preventing, prevention, warding off; a preventive
s.m. A small tambourine.
s.f. (dim.) = ḍaphlā, q.v.
s.f. (dim.) = H ڐفلا डफला ḍaflā, s.m. = ḍaphlā, q.v.
s.m. Burying, burial, interment; covering, hiding, concealing — dafn karnā, v.t. To bury; to hide, conceal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دفن (dafn), from Arabic دَفْن (dafn).

ety: dafn, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. aug.)+nā
def: v.t. To bury, &c. ( = dafn karnā).
wety: From دَفْن (dafn) + ـَانا (-ānā) by analogy with Classical Persian دَفْن کَرْدَن (dafn kardan). Compare Gujarati દફનાવવું (daphnāvvũ).

ety: A. daftar, diftar, fr. the P.; Gr. διφθερα 'a skin,' &c.
def: s.m. A roll, scroll, list; an index; a bundle of papers or written documents tied together in a cloth; a record, register, journal, book, volume, account-book; an official statement or report (especially of public revenue); archives; a record-office; an office, counting-house, place of business
subs: s.m. Head of an office; head clerk.

def: s.m. An office-keeper; a man who has charge of the stationery, &c. of an office, and whose business it is to arrange and bind the papers and books, rule sheets of paper, make and mend pens, prepare envelopes, &c.
s.f. The sides or boards (of the binding of a book); pasteboard.
s.f. The sides or boards (of the binding of a book); pasteboard.

ety: Zend dagha, fr. daz; S. दाह, rt. दह्, orig. दघ्; Prk. दाघ
def: s.m. A mark burnt in, a brand, cautery; mark, spot, speck; stain; stigma; blemish; iron-mould; freckle; pock; scar, cicatrix; wound, sore; grief, sorrow; misfortune, calamity; loss, injury, damage; — (adjectively) branded, cauterized, scarred, wounded, &c.
subs: dāg̠ bar bālāʼě dāg̠ = dāg̠-par dāg̠
s.f. Marking out the lines of a road with a spade; a line of road; mark
One misfortune following another, calamity on calamity
adj. Burnt; branded, cauterized; scarred; marked, spotted; streaked, striped; stained; blemished, spoiled
(-meṅ), To set fire (to), to set alight (as a pyre, &c.)
(-meṅ), To mark, to scar; to wound, to grieve
(-meṅ), To vilify, &c. = dāg̠ lagānā (-meṅ), To stain, sully, vilify, defame
(meṅ), To be burnt; to be branded; to be defamed, to get a bad name; to be damaged
v.n. To be cauterized, be scarred, &c.

def: s.m. = H داگنا दागना dāgnā, v.t. corr. of dāg̠; and dāg̠nā, qq.v.
s.f. Pace, step, stride (also written ḍheṅg, deg)
subs: ḍag dharnā, v.n. To step, step out, stride.

ety: prob. connected with dag̠l and dag̠dag̠a
def: s.f. Deceit, imposture, treachery; cheat, fraud, artifice, delusion
subs: adj. & s.m. Deceitful, treacherous, fraudulent; — cheat, knave, impostor, traitor
s.f. Deceitfulness, fraud, imposture, cheating, knavery, treachery, foul play, villainy
s.f. The being deceived or imposed on; — deceit, treachery
(-se, or ke sāth), To act deceitfully or treacherously, to practise deceit or treachery, to play false, &c.
(-se), To be deceived or imposed on (by).

ety: dagadh˚ = S. दग्ध, p.p.p. of rt. दह्
def: v.t. To burn, scorch, singe; to hurt, wound; to tease, vex, torment, pester; to chide, upbraid, threaten; to revile.
s.f. Pri e paid for branding or marking.

ety: v.n. fr. دغل; used in P. with amplification of signification
def: s.m. Corrupt state, corruptness, unsoundness; corruption, vice, depravity; deceit, fraud, treachery, villainy ( = dag̠ā); base coin; — a cheat, impostor, villain; a pickpocket; confusedness or entanglement of plants or herbage; abundance or luxuriance of herbage; luxuriant, tangled, or dense herbage; — adj. Unsound; base (as coin); corrupt, depraved; false, insincere, deceitful, treacherous
adj. Deceitful, treacherous; — dag̠alī or dag̠lī, s.f. Deceit, fraud, treachery, villainy ( = dag̠al).
s.m. A combination of four short syllables.
v.t. To cause to be, or to have, branded or marked; — to cause (a gun) to be fired.
s.m. A kettle-drum (cf. ḍaṅkā and ḍhakkā).

r: ḍagar, ḍagrā, ḍag, ḍagar batānā, ko, aur ḍagar ćalnā
def: s.f. = H ڐگرا डगरा ḍagrā, s.m. (fr. ḍag, q.v.), Highway, way, road, path, track; a wide path between fields (for the passage of cattle, &c.)
subs: (-ko), To show or point out the road, to direct, to guide
'To take another road or path'; to mislead, deceive, trick, cheat.

def: v.n. To rock; to roll, &c. ( = ḍagarnā, q.v.); — v.t. To doubt, discredit, disbelieve (a true testimony, &c.).
s.m. (local), A pole on which a load is suspended.
v.n. To go or walk along a road, to travel, to roam; to go away, take one's departure, be off; to roll; to totter
subs: v.n. To totter along, to walk with tottering steps; to roll.

ety: by redupl. of ḍag; see ḍignā
def: adj. & s.m. Shaking, tottering, trembling, &c. ( = ḍag-mag; and ḍagmagāhaṭ, qq.v.).

ety: S. दघ्, for दह्, by redupl.+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Burning, shining, bright; — s.m. A burning lamp (placed within a paper cup).

ety: fr. A. دغدغة dag̠dag̠at, inf. n. f. of دغدغ 'to tickle,' &c.; used in P. with amplification of signification
def: s.m. 'Titillation'; inclination or prompting (of the mind); suspicion, doubt, distrust; mental disturbance, tumult, perturbation, perplexity, anxiety, solicitude, care; alarm, fear, dread, trepidation, awe.

ety: fr. A. دغدغة dag̠dag̠at, inf. n. f. of دغدغ 'to tickle,' &c.; used in P. with amplification of signification
def: s.m. 'Titillation'; inclination or prompting (of the mind); suspicion, doubt, distrust; mental disturbance, tumult, perturbation, perplexity, anxiety, solicitude, care; alarm, fear, dread, trepidation, awe.
s.f. Brilliance, splendour; sparkling, sparkle, glow, twinkling, twinkle (as of a star).
= H ڐگڐگاکے डगडगाके ḍagḍagāke, = H ڐگدگانا डगडगाना ḍagḍagānā, See ḍakdakākar, and dakdakānā.
= H ڐگڐگاکر डगडगाकर ḍagḍagākar, = H ڐگدگانا डगडगाना ḍagḍagānā, See ḍakdakākar, and dakdakānā.

ety: dagdag˚ = S. दह् (or दघ्), redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To burn brightly, to shine, glow, gleam, twinkle; — to be inflamed, be very red.
v.n. To shake, totter, &c. ( = ḍagmagānā, q.v.).
= H ڐگڐگاکر डगडगाकर ḍagḍagākar, = H ڐگڐگاکے डगडगाके ḍagḍagāke, See ḍakdakākar, and dakdakānā.
v.n. To burn brightly, &c. ( = dhakdhakānā, q.v.).
s.f. = dagdagāhaṭ, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Burnt, consumed by fire, scorched, singed; cauterized; hurt, wounded, sore, grieved; unlucky, unpropitious; cunning
subs: s.m. A black crow, a raven.

def: adj. = dagdha, q.v.
s.m. lit. 'Taking long strides,' or 'having long legs'; longshanks; a horse, &c. that takes long steps or strides, a lean long-legged horse, a Rosinante (also written ḍhaggā; cf. ḍheṅkā).
adj. Marked, spotted, stained; branded, sullied, disgraced, dishonoured; spoiled, damaged
subs: v.t. To spot, to stain; to blemish; to brand, stigmatize, disgrace, dishonour.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Marked, spotted, stained ( = dāg̠-dār).
adj. Deceitful, &c. ( = dag̠al).
adj. Deceitful, &c. ( = dag̠al).
s.m. = دگلا daglā, q.v.
s.m. A coat or vest (angarkhā) padded with cotton, a quilted coat or vest.

r: ḍagmag, ḍigmag, ḍigmig, ḍagmagā, ḍigmagā, ḍag-ḍag
def: adj. Shaking, shaky, tottering, trembling, tremulous, rolling, reeling, swaying from side to side; unsteady, wavering, vacillating.

r: ḍagmagā, ḍigmagā, ḍag-ḍag, ḍagmag, ḍigmag, ḍigmig
def: adj. Shaking, shaky, tottering, trembling, tremulous, rolling, reeling, swaying from side to side; unsteady, wavering, vacillating.
s.f. Shaking, tottering, trembling, swaying, rolling; shivering, quaking.
v.n. = ḍagmagānā, q.v.
v.n. To shake, totter, tremble, stagger, reel; to shiver, quake, quail (with fear); to sway or roll (in walking), to move with a twisting wanton gait, to walk or move affectedly.

ety: dāg̠, q.v.+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To mark by burning with a hot iron, to brand, to cauterize; — to fire (a gun, &c.)
v.t. = H داگ दाग dāg, s.m. corr. of dāg̠; and dāg̠nā, qq.v.
v.n. To be branded with a hot iron, to be marked; — to be fired off (as a gun).
s.m. An impostor.
s.m. Road, way, highway ( = ḍagar).

r: ḍagrā, ḍagar, ḍag, ḍagar batānā, ko, aur ḍagar ćalnā
def: s.m. = H ڐگر डगर ḍagar, s.f. (fr. ḍag, q.v.), Highway, way, road, path, track; a wide path between fields (for the passage of cattle, &c.)
subs: (-ko), To show or point out the road, to direct, to guide
'To take another road or path'; to mislead, deceive, trick, cheat.

def: s.m. A round-bottomed basket or tray (made of split bamboo; used to hold rice, flour, &c., and also to winnow with).
v.t. To rock; to roll; to propel ( = ḍagrānā).
v.t. To put in motion, to propel, to roll.
s.f. dim. of dagrā, q.v.

ety: Prk. दाहो; S. दाहः, rt. दह्
def: s.f. Burning; combustion, conflagration; cauterizing; cautery; — fire; the sensation of burning, internal heat, feverish or morbid heat; inflammation; — ardour; heart-burning, envy, jealousy
subs: (-ko), To apply fire (to), to light (the funeral pile)
v.n. To bear envy, &c., to be envious or jealous; to grudge
s.m. A place where dead bodies are burnt
s.f. The act, or the ceremony, of burning a corpse
s.m. 'Removing heat'; the root of the fragrant grass Andropogon muricatus (which is woven into screens and kept wet for the purpose of tempering the hot winds; see ḵẖas-ḵẖas).

ety: Prk. दहो; S. द्रहः, prob. a Prākrit form of S. ह्रदः
def: s.m. Very deep water, a deep pool; depth, abyss; pool or eddy (in a river), whirlpool, vortex; a lake; a river (e.g. kaṅwal-dah, 'A pool or lake of lotuses or water-lilies'; kālī-dah, 'the river Kālī').

ety: Pehl. dah; Zend daśan, nom. daśa; S. दशन्, दश
def: adj. Ten (also used, for daś or das, by some of the old Hindī poets, as Ćand, &c., and in Hindī compounds)
subs: (bāshī, fr. T. bāsh, 'head'), s.m. A commander of ten men; a person having a place of trust; a valet
s.f. A ten-fold (return, — used in returning a salutation)
dah dar dunyā ṣad dar āḵẖirat

prov. 'Ten in this world, a hundred in the next'; charity will be returned ten-fold in the next world (used as a benediction by mendicants; — sattar, 'seventy,' is sometimes substituted for ṣad, 'hundred')
'Ten by ten,' ten cubic yards (of water, — such being regarded as pure according to the ḥadīs̤ or Mohammadan law)
adj. 'Having ten hearts'; fickle, inconstant, faithless; — brave, courageous, bold
s.m. An aggregate of ten days, a decade
adj. Of or for ten years; — s.f. A book comprising the collections, accounts, registers, &c. of ten years
s.m. A weight of ten ser or seers
adj. & s.m. Consisting of, or capable of carrying, ten men; — a company or body of ten men; a cart (smaller than a gāṛī or ćhakṛā), capable of carrying ten men
adj. Holding ten man or maunds
s.f. lit. 'Half of ten'; five per cent.
s.m., or dah-yakī, s.f. A tenth part, a tithe, one in ten; an allowance of ten per cent.

ety: Prk. डाहो or दाहो; S. दाहः, rt. दह्; — or fr. ḍāhnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Burning, rage, heart-burning, jealousy, envy, malice, spite, rancour

s.m. The first ten days of the Moharram (syn. ʻashara; ʻāshūrā); — the models of the tombs of Hasan and Husain, or the model of a building containing the tombs, which are carried about in procession during those ten days. (On the tenth day the tombs are buried, or in some places, thrown into water: the building, if made of cheap materials, shares the same fate; if costly, it is carried back and deposited in the dargāh or Karbalā.)
s.m. corr. of dahā, q.v.
s.f. The figure ten; tenth part; the tens, the tens' place (in numeration), the decimal place (of figures), a decimal.
s.m. corr. of dihez, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Burning, kindling, setting on fire; causing heat or combustion; incendiary; inflammatory; cauterizing, caustic; — an incendiary; — the plant Plumbago zeylanica.
s.f. Burning, blazing, conflagration; glow; ardour, fervency, fervour.
s.m. Cave, cavern; abyss; pitfall.
s.m. The place of tens (in Numeration).
s.m. A collection or aggregate of ten; — the tens' place, the tens (in Numeration).
s.m. Loss, damage; blow, shock; grief, sorrow, affliction.

ety: dahak˚ = Prk. दहक्क(इ), or दहक्के(इ), fr. S. दह+कृ
def: v.n. To burn, to blaze; to be burnt; to be ruined or destroyed, to be lost; to regret, grieve, be consumed with regret, &c.
v.t. = ḍahkānā, q.v.

s.f. Shaking tremulously, shaking, trembling, quaking, tremor; fear, apprehension, dread; — shifting sand, quicksand, &c. ( = daldal). (The name of Dahlī or Dehlī is said to be derived from this word; the ground on which the city was built being so loose and infirm that tent-pins could not be fixed in it.)

ety: dahal˚, prob. = Prk. दहड = S. दह+ल or र
def: v.n. To shake, tremble, quake; to fear, be alarmed or terrified
s.m. A collection or aggregate of ton (e.g. tīn dahām tīs).
adj. Burnt to ashes, burnt out, extinguished.
s.m. Causing to burn, setting fire to, burning, inflaming; cauterizing.
s.m. The mouth; an orifice.

s.m. (but f. in Hindī), Fire; burning, combustion, conflagration; sultriness; — s.m. Agni, the god of fire; lead-wort, Plumbago zeylanica; the marking nut, Anacardium officinarum; — a gold coin (in value six rupees)
subs: (˚na+up˚), s.m. 'Fire-stone'; the sun-gem; a crystal lens

def: s.m. contrac. of dahān, q.v.
s.m. Mouth (of a water-bag, or drain, or watercourse, or river, &c.), opening (of a valley, &c.); — bit (of a bridle); anything which covers the mouth.
v.t. To set fire to, to burn
s.m. See s.v. dahan.
s.m-1˚ = ḍagar, q.v.; — 2˚ = ḍahrā, q.v.
s.f. Roaring, thundering; roar, loud noise or cry
subs: dahāṛeṅ mār-kar ronā, To cry aloud, to weep bitterly ( = dāṛh mār-kar ronā).

def: s.m.? = dahrī, and ḍahrī, q.v.
s.m. = dehir, q.v.
s.m.f. Astonishment, amazement, consternation; — adj. Astonished, amazed, stupefied, confounded, bewildered, struck with consternation.
adj. Burning, scorching.
adj. (f. -ī), Glowing, bright, blooming, flourishing, fresh, verdant, green, pleasant, pleasing; overpowering, excessive, great.
v.n. To glow, shine; to bloom, blossom, flourish.
s.f. (m.?) = daldal, q.v.
s.m. pl. = dahā, q.v.
s.m. corr. of dihez, q.v.
s.m. = daheṛ, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.), The ten (at cards).
s.f. = dihlī or děhlī, q.v.
s.f. A vessel in which milk is coagulated, or in which coagulated milk is kept.
s.m. A vessel for carrying coagulated or sour milk (especially at festivals).
s.f. A vessel in which milk is coagulated, or in which coagulated milk is kept.

ety: prob. fr. S. दग्ध; but cf. ḍheṛ
def: s.m. A raven; a crow; a species of bird, Coracias; — the dial bird of Southern India, Copsychus saularis (a beautiful songster common throughout India).
s.f. = daheṅḍī, q.v.
adj. Of or belonging to a dowry; — flimsy, gim-crack.
adj. (f. -inī), Burning, setting on fire; burning hot; tormenting, paining.

ety: act. part. n. of دهی 'to possess intelligence'
def: adj. Possessing intelligence or sagacity, cunning, knowing, skilful, shrewd, penetrating.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Jealous, envious, malicious, spiteful, rancorous; — a malicious person, &c.
s.m. Thick sour milk, coagulated milk (called 'tire or tyre' in India)
subs: v.n. To be or become coagulated (milk)
(f. -ī), A seller of dahī or coagulated milk.

def: adj. Right (hand, &c.) = dahinā, q.v.
adj. (f.-ī), Right (hand, or side, &c.)
subs: dahnc obl. of dahnā; Prk. dāhiṇe; S. dakshiṇe)
adv. To the right; on the right; at the right.

def: part. Burnt, scorched.
v.t. To lead astray, to mislead; to deceive, disappoint, baulk, tantalize, &c. ( = bahkānā, q.v.).
v.t. To throw away uselessly, to spoil (cf. ḍahnā; ḍhānā).
v.t. To burn, kindle; to heat (iron, &c.), to inflame; to cause to regret or grieve; to ruin, destroy, lose.

ety: dah = das+lā = alā = Prk. अडओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. Ten (at cards), the ten (of a suit of cards).
s.m. Name of a tribe of Jagas in the Upper Doāb.
s.m. corr. of dalān, q.v.
v.t. To shake, to agitate; to cause to tremble or quake.

ety: = S. देहली; — A. dihlīz, fr. the Pers.
def: s.f. Threshold; portico, entrance hall, vestibule
subs: s.m. The dog of the porch, house-dog; — a hanger-on, parasite
s.m. lit. 'Treading the threshold'; a ceremonial visit paid by a young man about to be married to the parents of his intended bride
(-kī), To cross the threshold (of), to pay a visit (to).

ety: dāh˚ = Prk. दाह(इ) or दाहे(इ) = S. दाहय(ति), caus. fr. rt. दह्;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To set fire to, to burn, to kindle.

ety: Prk. दहणआं = S. दहनीयं, rt. दह्
def: v.n. To be burnt; to be inflamed; to burn, blaze.
v.n. = ḍhanā or ḍhahnā, q.v.

ety: ḍāh˚ = Prk. डाह(इ) or डाहे(इ), fr. डाह = S. दाह
def: v.n. To be hot, to burn; to burn with rage or envy, &c., to be malicious, or spiteful; to be heated or fused (as metals).
v.n. (Braj.) = dahnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. To be burnt, fit to be burnt; combustible; — anything to be burnt.
s.m. A measure of about ten handfuls (of grain, &c.).
s.m. Tenth part, tithe, ten per cent., an allowance or tax of ten per cent.

s.m. Time; a long period of time; an age; eternity; fortune, fate; chance, adverse fortune, misfortune, calamity, adversity; danger; — custom, habit, mode, manner; care, solicitude; the world; atheism.

s.m. A hole dug for water; hollow or low ground (in which water collects), tank, &c. ( = ḍabrā, ḍābar, q.v.); a water-tank (in a ship), a barrel or cask in which drinking water is kept (in a boat or ship); the part of a boat or ship in which bilge-water collects.
adj. Relating to time, or to fortune; secular, temporal; relating to the world, worldly; impious, atheistical.
s.f. Stiff clay soil (in low ground), marshy soil.
adj. Marshy; — s.f. Marshy soil, &c. ( = ḍabrā).
s.m. = دهريه dahrīya, q.v.

ety: for A. دهرية, fr. dahrī, q.v.
def: s.m. One who maintains the eternity of matter, a materialist, an atheist.
adj. & s.f. See s.v. dah, 'ten.'

ety: for A. دهشة, fr. dahash, q.v.
def: s.f. Consternation, fear, fright, terror, dismay, dread, awe, alarm
subs: adj. Horrible, terrible, hideous, terrifying, alarming; threatening, intimidating
(-ko), To frighten, terrify, alarm, &c., to instil dread (into)
adj. Terror-stricken, panic-struck, terrified, frightened, alarmed, scared
(-se), To be frightened, be afraid, be intimidated, &c.
adj. Alarming, terrifying, &c. ( = dahshat-angez); frightened, &c. ( = dahshat-zada).

def: (dialec.); 1˚ = dāh; — 2˚ = dāhī; — 3˚ = dāʼū; qq.v.
s.m. A gallinule.

ety: Pehl. dahum; Zend daśema; S. दशम
def: adj. Tenth.

ety: act. part. n. of دعو 'to supplicate,' &c.
def: s.m. One who prays for the welfare of another, a well-wisher; — a plaintiff, complainant; — one who instigates (another to anything), instigator; author, cause; motive, means.
adj. & s.m. Giving, bestowing; giver, &c. (com. used at the end of comp., e.g. dukh-dāʼī, q.v.).

ety: Prk. दत्तिआ; S. दात्रिका rt. दा
def: s.f. A nurse; a wet-nurse; a midwife; (local) a maid-servant; — the stand that children run to in playing hide-and-seek
subs: s.f. A superior maid-servant, a lady's maid
v.t. To put out to nurse

def: s.f. A gift.
s.m. = dāʼī, 'giver,' q.v.
s.m.f. The Deity; a god; destiny, fate; — s.f. Godhead, divinity; — intj. O God! my God! (cf. Fr. mon Dieu!)
subs: s.m. 'A god's peer'; a demon, devil ( = deʼo)
(-ko), To be visited by divine wrath, to be unfortunate
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Struck by the Deity, fate-stricken, accursed; — an accursed one.

def: s.f. 1˚ = dayā (e.g. nir-daʼī); — 2˚ = daiyā, qq.v.
part. (dialec.) = dī, q.v.
s.m. The tenth month of the solar year (when the sun is in the sign Capricorn), December; the beginning of winter, winter.
adj. & adv. = daivī, q.v.
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: fr. A. dāj, rt. دجو 'to be dark,' by imāla+P. aff. war or ūr; — A. also daijūr
def: adj. & s.m. Dark, pitch dark, black; — the night of conjunction of sun and moon; a dark night without moonlight; — anything black; darkness, obscurity.

ety: act. part. n. of دام (for دوم
def: adj. & adv. Continuing, lasting; continuing always, continual, &c. (see dāʼimī, the more com. form.); — continually, always, perpetually ( = dāʼimaṉ)
subs: adj. & s.m. Perpetually drinking wine; — a sot, a drunkard

def: adv. Continually, perpetually, for ever.

adj. Continual, lasting, enduring, permanent, durable, standing; prolonged; perpetual, in perpetuity, continuing always or for ever, unintermittent (as fever), ceaseless, eternal, everlasting; — s.f. Permanence; perpetuity, &c. ( = dāʼimīyat, q.v.).

ety: for A. دائمية, abst. s. fr. dāʼimī
def: s.f. Continuance, durableness, permanence; perpetuity, eternity.
s.m. One who borrows, or takes upon credit; a debtor; — one who gives credit, a creditor.

ety: S. धामन्, rt. धा; or ध्यामन्
def: s.f. Equality (of age or size); measure, size
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of equal age or size; — an equal (in age or size).

def: adj. Relating to a day, diurnal, daily.
s.m. Poverty, wretchedness, misery, miserable condition; humbleness, lowliness; meanness; covetousness.
s.m. Wing (of a bird).
s.m. A debt; a loan (syn. qarẓ)
subs: s.f. The certificate given to a banker who has given up his effects
s.m. A debt payable on demand
s.m. A debt of which payment is deferred.

def: s.m. pl. (of dūn, formed after the model of the Arabic), Hill estates.
s.m. Wing (of a bird); branch, bough (of a tree).
s.f. (prov.) = dāʼī, q.v.
s.f. The middle finger.
adj. Diurnal, daily ( = dain).
s.f. A day's hire or wages.
s.m. = dait, daitya, q.v.
s.m. Poverty, &c. ( = dain, and dīnatā, qq.v.).

ety: act. part. n. of دار (for دور
def: part. adj. Going round, encircling; in process, agitated (as a law-suit); capable of being agitated; cognizable
subs: adj. Proceeding, under consideration or trial, pending
v.t. To institute (a suit), to put in (a complaint)
adj. Going the circuit (judges, &c.); absolute, invested with authority.

def: s.m. A convent or monastery (of Christians, or of Sūfīs, &c.); a temple, a place of worship, a church.

ety: for A. دائرة, fr. dāʼir, q.v.
def: s.m. Circuit, compass, circumference; a ring, a circle; orbit; — a large tambourin; — a monastery
subs: s.m. The chief of a monastery, an abbot; a prior

def: adj. Of or appertaining to an idol temple, &c. (see dair).
s.m. 'Binder of the Daityas,' an epithet of Vishṇu.
s.m. A son of Diti, &c. ( = daitya, q.v.); — adj. Proceeding from or belonging to the Daiteyas; sprung from Diti.
s.m. 'A son of Diti,' a being hostile to the gods, a demon, an evil spirit, a Titan, giant (of the Hindū mythology)
subs: s.m. An age of the demons (consisting of 12,000 divine years or the sum of the four Yugas or ages of man)
s.m.f. The place or source of demons.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Of or belonging to a deity or divinity; coming from the gods, divine, celestial; — s.m. One of the forms of marriage, the gift of a daughter at a sacrifice to the officiating priest; — a deity, a celestial being; — a religious offering or rite, an oblation or offering to the gods; — divine power, destiny, fate, fortune, chance, accident; the part of the hand sacred to the gods, i.e. the tips of the fingers
subs: s.f. Providential dispensation, sudden misfortune, unforeseen disaster, accident
vulg. daivaggya, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Acquainted with fate, knowing the destinies of men, foretelling; — an astrologer, fortune-teller; a prophet
vulg. daiv-gyān, s.m. Knowledge of the destinies of men, supernatural knowledge
adj. Connected with a deity, or with fate
s.m. Offering oblations to the gods, sacrificing, worship of the gods, a religious rite ( = dev-karm)
daiv-ghaṭnā, s.f. Course of destiny, run of fate, an event for which the gods (rather than men) are answerable, fortune, lot; misfortune, accident
s.f. A voice from heaven, oracle, divine revelation
s.m. An age of the gods, cycle of the immortals
s.m. A fortuitous combination, the intervention of destiny, the occurrence of any unforeseen event, fortune, chance, accident, contingency
adj. Worthy of chance or fortune.

def: adj. = daiv, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. daiv.
s.m. = daivajna, q.v.s.v. daiv.
s.m. See s.v. daiv.
s.m. (f. -ikā), An astrologer; an almanac-maker.
adv. By the interposition of fate, fatally, providentially, by chance, fortuitously, accidentally.
adj. Of or relating to a god or to the gods, belonging to a deity; divine; s.m. A god, a deity, an idol of a god; the deities
subs: s.m. 'Lord of the gods,' an epithet of Indra.

def: adj. Divine; wonderful (see daiv); — s.f. An inevitable accident, &c. ( = daivāgati); — adv. By chance, &c.; at length (see daivāt)
s.f. A female astrologer; an astrologer's wife.
adj. Of or relating to the gods or to fate.
s.m. Divine power or effect, fate, fortune, chance.
s.f. Mother; — the stand that children run to in playing hide and seek
subs: s.f. 'A cry for mother'; a cry for help; a loud noise, outcry.

ety: for A. داعية dāʻiyat, fem., v.n. fr دعو
def: s.m. Claim, pretention; demand; suit, petition, plaint, complaint; desire, wish.
s.m. = daheṛ, q.v.
s.m. = ديتيه daitya, q.v.

ety: fr. ديث 'to be soft, or easy'
def: s.m. A wittol, a tame cuckold; one who winks at the adultery of his wife or the fornication of his female relations from interested motives.
s.m. = daiyūs̤, q.v.
s.m. A dark night; darkness, obscurity.
s.m. A liar, a great deceiver; — the false Christ, Antichrist; — adj. & s.m. Having one eye; — a one-eyed man.
s.f. = dākh, q.v.
s.m. Woollen cloth from which hairs hang, a coarse kind of woollen cloth (such as that of which a faqīr's blanket is made); — a fine and precious sort of cloth.

ety: prob. fr. S. द्राक् 'quickly,' or v.n. of ḍāknā, q.v.
def: s.f. Post (for conveyance of letters), the mail; disposition or relay of horses, or runners, or pālkī-bearers along a road (for conveyance of the post or travellers); travelling by post; a post-office
subs: s.f. A post station, a station or stage at which a relay (of horses, or runners, &c.) is posted (for conveyance of the mail, or travellers)
s.m. Expenses incurred in travelling by post; postal charges; postage
s.m. A horse used for a ḍāk or relay; (fig.) a worn-out horse; — a fast walker
s.f. Mail-cart; mail-train; a carriage used in travelling by ḍāk
(-kī), To post relays (of horses, or runners, or pālkī-bearers)
v.n. A post to be organized; a ḍāk or relay (of horses, &c.) to be laid
adv. In the manner of the post, postingly, post-haste, without stopping on the way.

ety: prob. S. दाहकः; rt. दह्; cf. ḍahnā
def: s.m. A piece of gold or silver leaf, &c. placed under a gem (in a ring) to heighten its brilliance, a foil; a coloured substance placed under glass to form a mock gem ( = ḍāṅk).
s.m. The husband of a ḍākinī, q.v.; a wizard, sorcerer.
s.m. = ḍaṅk, q.v.
s.m. Giver, donor, one who makes presents (esp. to Brāhmans); the institutor of a sacrifice who employs and pays the officiating priests.
s.f. Continual vomiting; — calling out, shouting, &c.; bidding (at an auction)

ety: Prk. डक्कओ or डक्कअं; S. दष्ट+कः or कं
def: s.m. Robbery, gang-robbery; an attack by a band of robbers; a band or gang of robbers
subs: v.n. An attack to be made by robbers or 'dacoits,' 'dacoits' to fall (upon)
ḍākā ḍālnā, To commit 'dacoity' or robbery in gangs

def: s.m. pl. (of daqīqa), Subtleties, nice points; difficulties; minutiæ; minutes, moments; — flour, meal (in this sense used in P. for the A. sing. daqīq).
s.m. = ḍakait, q.v.

ety: ḍākā, q.v.+ait = āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्, the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. aug. āpi
def: s.m. One of a gang of robbers, a 'dacoit,' robber, brigand, highwayman, pirate ( = ḍākū).
s.f. = ḍakaitī, q.v.

ety: the preceding+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Gang-robbery, 'dacoity,' highway-robbery, robbery, piracy.
s.f. pl. (of dukkān), Shops.
s.f. A belch, eructation; — roaring, roar (of a tiger, &c.); bellowing, lowing (of an ox)
subs: 'Not to belch,' to make no noise, give or make no sign, to keep one's own counsel, not to betray oneself.

ety: Prk. डक्कडी and क्कडडओ; S. दष्ट+र, and र +कः
def: A strong, rich, clayey soil (regarded as the best, or second best, quality of soil; but requiring much ram to saturate it sufficiently for sowing).

ety: ḍakār˚ = S. उद्गार, rt. गृ with उद्
def: v.n. 'To belch'; to roar, bellow, low, make a loud noise; — v.t. To take down with a noise, to gulp down, to swallow; to embezzle
subs: ḍakār-jānā (with acc.), To swallow up; to embezzle, make away with, misappropriate.

def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs descended from a Brāhmin on the father's and a gwālin on the mother's side (they subsist on alms asked on Saturdays, and are skilled in astrology and fortune-telling, &c.); an individual of this caste.

s.m. A caste of Hindūs descended from a Brāhmin on the father's and a gwālin on the mother's side (they subsist on alms asked on Saturdays, and are skilled in astrology and fortune-telling, &c.); an individual of this caste.
adv. With loud or large gulps, in great draughts
subs: v.t. To drink with loud gulps, or in large draughts.

def: adv. With loud or large gulps, in great draughts
subs: v.t. To drink with loud gulps, or in large draughts.

def: v.n. = ḍagḍagānā, q.v.

ety: by redupl. of ḍak; see ḍakār; and cf. ḍhoknā
def: v.n. To make a noise in swallowing, to take large or loud gulps; to drink great draughts.
s.f. Grape; a raisin
adj. & s.m. Good; fit, able, qualified; — company, throng, army, row (of men); — essence (of anything).

ety: act. part. n. of دخل 'to enter,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Entering, penetrating; arriving; — entered; inserted; included, comprehended; produced; forthcoming; — interior, inside; — entering (upon), taking possession (of); — entry (in a book or account); inclusion (of a minor in a larger piece of land, &c.)
subs: s.m. 'Inserting and striking out'; a transfer of land or property under one name to another name in a deed or register; erasure of an entry; — dāḵẖil-ḵẖārij karnā, To transfer land or property (by taking out the name of the former proprietor from a deed or register and inserting that of the new)
adj. Entered upon the record, placed on record; filed with the record; laid aside, or indefinitely postponed; struck off the file
v.t. To file with the record, &c.

(-meṅ, or -ke andar), To cause to enter or be entered; to place or fix (in); to cause to arrive; to produce; to incorporate, include; to admit, lodge; to enter, insert, introduce; to enrol, enlist; to file; to deposit, to carry to credit; to pay (in); — to effect penetration
dāḵẖil-kunanda, s.m. One who enters or inserts, &c.; one who pays (in), payer, depositor
s.m. A warrant or deed of possession; a conveyance

(-meṅ), To enter, to come or go (in); to arrive; to penetrate; to enter (upon), take possession (of), to occupy; to come (under), to belong or appertain (to); — to be entered, be inserted, be enrolled, &c.; to be filed; to be paid (in), to be deposited.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَاخِل (dāxil), borrowed from Arabic دَاخِل (dāḵil).

def: adj. & s.m. Allowed entrance, admitted; introduced, adopted (as a word); occupying, in possession (of land, &c.); — intimate, familiar; — meddling, interfering; — one who has entered on possession (of); — one who has a firm footing (in any business, post, or office, &c.); — an adventive abider (among a people); a guest; an intimate, a confidant; an accomplice; — one who interferes or intermeddles, a meddler
subs: adj. & s.m. In possession (of lands, &c.); — an occupant; — one who has authority to interfere
s.f. The being in possession (of), possession, occupation, occupancy
v.t. To allow (one) to enter (in, -meṅ), to admit; to put (one) in possession (of)
(-meṅ), To enter (upon), to occupy; to be introduced or adopted (as a word); to be a meddler, to meddle, interfere (in).

ety: for A. داخلة dāḵẖilat, fem. of dāḵẖil
def: adj. & s.m. Interior, inside; — entry (in an account, &c.); insertion; quotation; entrance; entrance fee; admittance, admission; delivery or payment (of money, &c.); a receipt (for money, — esp. for revenue paid in)

ety: for A. داخلة dāḵẖilat, fem. of dāḵẖil
def: adj. & s.m. Interior, inside; — entry (in an account, &c.); insertion; quotation; entrance; entrance fee; admittance, admission; delivery or payment (of money, &c.); a receipt (for money, — esp. for revenue paid in)
adj. Interior, internal; home (as opp. to foreign); — included, contained (in); inclusive; belonging (to); inherent
subs: s.m. A newly formed village attached to or included in an older (aṣlī) village.

def: adj. & s.m. Southern, south; — the south; the southern part of Hindūstān, the Deccan.
adj. Southern; of or belonging to southern India or the Deccan (generally applied to persons or things from the south); — dakhinī podīna, s.m. Horse-mint
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of دخل 'to come in'
def: s.m. Entrance, ingress, admission, access; entering (upon), taking possession (of), possession, occupation, occupancy; making way or progress (in a study), progress, proficiency, knowledge; reach, grasp, scope, comprehension; possibility; capacity, competency; influence, power, authority, jurisdiction; — intrusion, interference, meddling, disturbance, molestation; — income, receipt, revenue, profit, produce, proceeds
subs: v.n. To have access, to be admitted; to obtain possession, be put in possession (of)
s.m. One who has admission (to); a partner, an accomplice
(-ko), To give admission (to), to allow to enter; to interpose (in or between, -meṅ), to interfere; to interrupt, to disturb
(-par), To enter (upon), take possession (of), to occupy; to encroach (upon); — to meddle (in, -meṅ), intermeddle, interfere (in or with), concern (oneself), intrude; to interrupt
s.m. An occupier, occupant; an encroacher, an intruder, &c.
v.t. To keep possession of, to retain
s.m. A document giving the right of occupancy, a writ or warrant of entrance or of possession (of lands, &c.)
s.f. Obtaining entrance or admission; taking possession
v.t. To exclude; to dispossess, dislodge, eject

def: s.m. The house for the reception of the dead among the Gabrs or fire-worshippers; a vault or tomb for the dead; a coffin; — a substance like lights which a male camel protrudes from the mouth when in heat.
s.f. A south wind ( = dakhnā).
adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, voracious; — a glutton.
adj. Slender, small, minute, fine, thin (opp. to g̠alīz̤); paltry, trifling, inconsiderable; subtile, nice, abstruse, recondite, obscure, difficult; — s.m. Flour, meal; farina.

ety: for A. دقيقة daqīqat, fem. of daqīq
def: s.m. Anything small or minute, a particle; an inconsiderable or trifling business or matter; a subtile question, abstruse or nice point; — a minute, a moment of time
subs: adj. Comprehending nice or abstruse points, penetrating, discerning; of quick apprehension.

ety: fem. of ḍākiya; -in = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. A female robber; the wife of a 'dacoit' or robber; — a postman's wife.
s.f. = ḍākinī, q.v.

ety: Prk. डक्किणी, डाकिणी; S. डाकिनी, दंष्ट्रिणी
def: s.f. A kind of female demon, a female imp, a witch whose evil eye causes children to pine and die (and who is believed to consume their livers); — (met.) a termagant, a shrew.
s.m. Postman ( = ḍāk-wālā, q.v.).
s.m. = ḍākū, q.v.
s.m. One who bruises, brays, or pounds; a corn-grinder.

ety: Prk. दक्खिणओ and दक्खिणा; S. दक्षिण+कः and दक्षिणा
def: s.f. Southern; — a southerly wind.
s.m. = dakshiṇāyan, q.v.s.v. dakshiṇ

ety: ḍāk˚ = S. उद्+वम्+कृ; cf. ḍoknā, ḍoṅknā
def: v.n. To vomit; — to call out, bawl, shout, vociferate, roar, bellow (cf. ḍakārnā, and daḍūknā).

ety: prob. A. دكّ and لق; also written dak-o-lak, dag̠-o-lag̠, laq-o-daq, &c.
def: s.m. An extensive uncultivated plain; a desert.

ety: ḍak or ḍaṅk, q.v.+Prk. उल्लिआ = S. उल+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.), A wasp; a hornet.

ety: prob. fr. S. दंश;+rā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Venom, poison; a kind of medicament or drug.

ety: Prk. डक्कडी and क्कडडओ; S. दष्ट+र, and र +कः
def: A strong, rich, clayey soil (regarded as the best, or second best, quality of soil; but requiring much ram to saturate it sufficiently for sowing).
v.n. To make a loud noise, to roar, bellow, low (as an ox); to cry aloud or bitterly.

adj. & s.m. Able, competent, fit, suitable; dexterous, deft, expert, adroit, skilful, clever; — an able, or clever, or dexterous person; a scholar, panḍit; — name of one of the Prajāpatis, sons of Brahmā
subs: (˚ksha+adh˚), s.m. 'Disturber of the sacrifice of Daksha'; an epithet of Śiva (Daksha having instituted a sacrifice to which he invited all the gods except his son-in-law Śiva and his wife Satī, the latter went unbid, and being received contemptuously, threw herself into the fire; upon which an emanation or incarnation of Śiva was produced named Vīra-bhadra, who attacked Daksha, and a general affray ensued: Daksha was decapitated, but was restored to life by Śiva at the prayer of the gods)
and daksha-kanyā, s.f. 'Daughter of Daksha,' Durgā (called also Pārvatī, Devī, Satī, &c.).

def: adj. Relating or appertaining to Daksha.
s.f. = S دکشتو दक्षत्व dakshatva, s.m. Ability, cleverness, dexterity.
s.m. = S دکشتا दक्षता dakshatā, s.f. Ability, cleverness, dexterity.

s.f. Any of the daughters of Daksha; — name of the nymph and asterism Rohiṇī; — the plant Croton polyandrum; — f. pl. The twenty-seven lunar mansions (considered mythologically as the daughters of Daksha and wives of the moon).
s.m. Son of Daksha
subs: s.f. Name of a village in the north of India in the country of the Vāhlikas or Balḵẖ
adj. & s.m. Relating to Dākshi-kanṭhā; produced or born in Dākshi-kanṭhā; — a native of Dākshi-kanṭhā.

def: adj. Able, fit, competent, well-qualified ( = daksh, q.v.).

s.f. The south ( = dakshiṇ, q.v.); — fee for spiritual services, a present to Brāhmans on occasions of sacrifice; donation, reward, alms (esp. to Brāhmans); — a figure of the goddess Durgā in which the right side is said to be advanced.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Right (opp. of left; see dahinā); southern, south; — able, competent, clever, skilful, dexterous, deft; — straightforward, candid, sincere, upright, honest; polite, affable, amiable, pleasing; — the right hand or side; the south; the southern part of Hindūstān
subs: (˚ṇa+āć˚), adj. & s.m. Honest or upright in conduct; — one who conducts himself honestly
(˚ṇa+ag˚), s.m. The southern fire of the altar, a sacred fire placed towards the south
dakshiṇ-mukh, adj. & adv. Having the face turned southward, facing the south; — s.m. One who turns his face towards the south
(˚ṇa+ay˚), s.m. The sun's progress south of the equator, southing, south-declination, the half-year in which the sun moves from north to south, the winter solstice
(˚nā+āv˚), adj. & s.m. Turning towards the right; turning to the south; — the south country, the Dakkhan; — a conch shell with the valve opening to the right
wety: From Sanskrit दक्षिण (dakṣiṇa), from Proto-Indo-European *deḱs-. Cognate with Lithuanian dešinė, Ancient Greek δεξιός (dexiós), Latin dexter.

def: s.f. Ability, skilfulness, cleverness, dexterity; uprightness, honesty; politeness, kindness.
adv. To the right hand, on the right; to the south, southward.
adj. Of or belonging to the south or the Dakkhan (a man, or horse, &c.); southern, south ( = dakhinī).
adj. Worthy of the sacrificial fee; meriting a reward; worthy to be honoured with presents.
